column1: # Classifications: accessor | constructor | derives | grandfathered | helper | loader | predicate
# One row per public function in the physics and materials substrate that does NOT carry a
# `// @derives:` marker, with what it is instead. A function that DOES carry a marker needs no row:
# `grandfathered` means the function predates the gate and nobody has classified it yet. It is not a
# a marker instead of a row.
# claim that the function does not derive; it is an admission that no one has looked. Classify it
# properly when the function is next touched, and if it turns out to derive a world quantity, give it
# the gate reads the marker directly, so the registry and this baseline cannot disagree.

column4: returns the tolerated offset from the anchors' measured pressure frame; zero while the reserved value conductivity.ambient_frame_pressure_slack_bar is unset, which is the absence of a tolerance rather than a derived one
the Bruggeman residual function the bisection solves; pure arithmetic with no world quantity of its own
finds the already-derived envelope covering a cell count by asking lattice_exponent_class for its class; a lookup into a list the caller computed, the derivation itself is derived_estimator_bands
returns the sources' own measured reference pressure, the 1 bar frame the cited kappa_298 rows and the banked Grueneisen rows both declare

reads where the cited calibration set places a cell count and with what exponent; returns the cited 0.33 for the complex class and names which gap it fell in otherwise, a read of a cited set rather than a derivation
the fixed-point bisection that finds the residual's root; the numerical method, not the quantity
Simpson quadrature of the dimensionless Debye integral; interval count bounds the step, not the count
the dimensionless integrand x^3/(e^x-1), series-continued through its removable singularity at the origin
assembles the six MGD anchors from one source fit, refusing a mixed-provenance or systematics-graded set
returns the volume held by either ExpansionEdge variant so the bracket can read it without matching; the spinodal itself is derived in expansion_edge, this only hands it back
Display formatter for ExpansivityRefusal; renders a refusal, derives nothing
absolute difference of two Fixed values; arithmetic, derives nothing
Display formatter for ThermoRefusal; renders a refusal
the elastic Debye temperature's kelvin value; one-way by design, no Fixed goes back in
delegates to mineral_moduli::canonical_phase_key; a name lookup, not a derivation
reports which rung-3 anchors are banked; answers a question about the DATA, derives no world quantity
reports the min/max envelope over branches already computed; a REPORTING ENVELOPE and never a distribution, since its interior holds no model, so it derives no world quantity
parses held decimal text exactly and applies the repository Q32.32 rounding rule; it changes representation but derives no world quantity
the odd dimensionless antiderivative of the symmetric line-load shape; pure quadrature, derives no world quantity
total two-sided envelope comparison that forms no negation, so it cannot panic at Fixed::MIN the way the abs form did
envelope check; derives the strip bound from MAX_LINE_LOAD_GPA_KM via V0 = q(2a) rather than authoring a ceiling
classifies a modulus pair as physically inadmissible, outside the declared proof envelope, or admissible; reads the envelope constants and the isotropic stability bounds, derives no world quantity
the thickness arm of the same two-class split: non-positive is not a plate, past MAX_ELASTIC_THICKNESS_KM is a plate the range proof does not reach
unit conversion: GPa km^3 into the typed InternalRigidity
wraps a raw internal number as InternalRigidity; crate-private so only the conversions and the solve may mint one
unwraps InternalRigidity to its Fixed for this crate's own arithmetic; the type exists so the unwrap cannot happen outside it
the isotropic stability bound on Poisson's ratio, minus one; where the material stops existing rather than where this kernel stops proving
unit conversion: the typed InternalRigidity back into GPa km^3, fallible because some real plates do not fit that unit
returns the solved plate's internal rigidity, typed; the carrier that exists for every solved plate including those rigidity_gpa_km3 cannot express
unwraps ElevationKm to its Fixed; the typed boundary exists so a downward deflection cannot be stored as an elevation, and this is the deliberate exit
unit readout of the axisymmetric length derived once in from_internal_rigidity
unit readout of the line-load flexural parameter derived once in from_internal_rigidity
returns the stored internal rigidity the plate was built from; no conversion and no derivation
the periodic spectral deflection profile over a closed row, from the per-cell load field, the plate rigidity and the restoring modulus
the periodic elevation profile: the periodic deflection through the ElevationKm boundary
the shared point-load coefficient and Kelvin argument the deflection and its truncation band both read; one home so a band cannot be computed from a different coefficient than the value it bands
whether a modulus pair sits inside the declared validation envelope
unit conversion: internal curvatures back into 1/km
unit conversion: internal lengths back into km
unit conversion: internal rigidities back into GPa km^3
unit conversion: GPa km^2 into internal forces
unit conversion: km/s^2 into internal accelerations
unit conversion: km into internal lengths (a division by 32)
unit conversion: GPa km into internal line loads
unit conversion: GPa km^3 into internal rigidities
whether a line-load intensity sits inside the declared validation envelope
the declared validation envelope's Poisson bound, one half
whether a point-load magnitude sits inside the declared validation envelope
the shared D/(delta_rho g) quotient both flexural lengths are quarter powers of
filters the candidate-audit records already loaded to report closure and authored rows; it admits no magnitude and computes no world quantity
returns an already-loaded candidate-audit record by id; it admits no magnitude and computes no world quantity
parses held decimal text exactly and applies the repository Q32.32 rounding rule; it changes representation but derives no world quantity
validates one law binding against an explicitly supplied kernel contract and returns only acceptance or a structural error; it computes no world quantity
merges already parsed floor manifest records into the registry while rejecting duplicate ids; it computes no world quantity
parses and loads a compatibility-owned floor while accepting an additional structural contract resolver; it computes no world quantity
checks registry cross-references graph acyclicity and kernel bindings against root plus compatibility contracts; it returns only acceptance or a structural error
Display formatter for PressureMeltingRefusal; renders a refusal and derives no world quantity
maps a PressureMeltingStage variant to its stable diagnostic label; derives no world quantity
D^(3/4) from an internal rigidity, without ever forming D
returns the two stored edge rigidities in the internal units they are held in; no conversion and no derivation
unit readout of the stored internal rigidity, fallible because `GPa km^3` cannot express a sluggish world's plate
reads one symbol from an already sealed physical execution capability
reads one noncausal value from the sealed SI representation schema
integer rounding policy used to form a lookup key; it derives no physical quantity
returns the cited crystallographic count for a phase

parses the cited [[conductivity]] blocks and RECONSTRUCTS atoms_per_primitive_cell from the row's own space group, Z, formula content and lattice centring, refusing on disagreement; a transcription check on cited data rather than a derivation of a world quantity
whether the table holds no rows
returns the cited measured anchor and its band for a phase, or None where no primary reports one
returns a cited row by canonical phase key
iterates every cited row in canonical key order, so a consumer needing a statistic across the column does not carry a hardcoded name list
loads the vendored cited phase_conductivity.toml column; no derivation, the reconstruction check lives in from_toml_str
reads a named value only from an admitted physical execution capability
reads only noncausal exact SI definitions and representation-derived relations
whether two source inversions agree; single-channel is NOT agreement, it is unchecked
whether every cell a row needs was fit rather than estimated from systematics
Display for the effective Debye temperature, naming which average it is
parses the [[anchor]] blocks; line-anchored split so header prose naming the marker cannot manufacture a row
the effective Debye temperature's kelvin value; one-way by design, no Fixed goes back in
whether the row's own fit reproduces the banked gamma_0, so the MGD set comes from one inversion
vocabulary parse for the cell-grade / channel-agreement / gamma-pairing enums; fatal on an unrecognised member rather than defaulting
iterates the loaded phase keys in deterministic order
one phase's loaded anchor row, or None; no default is ever supplied
whether a cell's grade permits reading it as an anchor (fit yes, systematics no)
reads one cited (alpha, gamma, beta) row from the vendored column; the derivation is laws::ln_stagnant_lid_nusselt, which carries the @derives marker
builds a Rayleigh number tagged with a definition the column itself declares; refuses a key no banked row uses, which is the guard rather than a quantity
the sibling constructor on the Frank-Kamenetskii axis
reads the definition key a projection was built with
reads the log-domain Rayleigh number a projection carries
reads the Frank-Kamenetskii parameter a projection carries
reports the envelope over determinations already computed by the marked stagnant_lid_determinations; a reporting hull, never a distribution
answers whether a convicted row could still be evaluated at all
answers whether a row carries both exponents, so is readable as a suppression law
parses a family's declared selection rule string into the implemented rule, refusing an unimplemented one
groups the stagnant-lid rows into the families their data declares, in file order
runs one row's scope tests and its own evaluation; the derivation is the marked stagnant_lid_determinations above it
checks one family's declaration and applies its rule to the preflighted members; part of the marked stagnant_lid_determinations
applies the family's own selection rule to members already evaluated; part of the marked stagnant_lid_determinations
one row at one coefficient through laws::ln_stagnant_lid_nusselt, which carries the @derives marker for the law itself
reads one row's cited provenance string, the human-readable half a determination's selected row points at
returns the single element of an iterator or the reason there is not exactly one; the guard against a silent first-match
returns the riders a scope verdict already carries, either way; a caveat never blocks and nothing is computed here
answers whether a scope verdict convicted the row; a question about the DATA's own declared reach, deriving no world quantity
reads G only after constructing and verifying the repository-owned absolute floor
reads the logarithm of G through the sealed execution capability
